title: STUDY THEME,  The Humanity of Jesus. Part 28
devotional:
Jesus’ resurrection teaches us that the power of God in our lives will restore and rebuild broken relationships.

Relationships are rescued when our lives are touched with the power of Jesus’ new life. Between his resurrection and ascension, the main focus of Jesus was one of restoring and reassuring. His disciples were in disarray, fear and confusion; they were not able to understand what was going on. So Jesus patiently took the time to gently rescue them from their fear and misunderstanding.

The life of Jesus clearly demonstrates that the power of the resurrection is completely free from any kind of abusive or controlling tendencies, and will empower someone with the humility to bow and help those who hearts have been crushed with guilt and shame. In John 20:17 we see that this is the first time that Jesus refers to the disciples as ‘brothers,’ in this gospel. After his resurrection Jesus inaugurated a new level of intimacy between himself and his disciples.

It is important to notice that every encounter Jesus had with people after his resurrection was initiated by him. Jesus went to those who were broken, fearful and doubting, he went to people where they were. He pursued those who disowned and forsook him. I know that in my own life, to my shame, I have often gone out of my way to avoid someone who had caused me pain. Sometimes it is right to do that, but not always. There is something in all of us that wants to keep our distance from those who have hurt us. When in our humanity. we experience the power of his resurrection we will be secure enough to reach out to those who have caused us pain. This is the real demonstration of the power of God. This is how God reveals his glory, when we initiate the process of restoring relationships.

In our humanity we need the power of God to sanctify our scars.

When Jesus appeared to his disciples, on two occasions he showed them his wounds, see. John 20:20,27. He displayed his scars with confidence, not disgrace or shame, The power of the resurrection did not remove Jesus’ scars, it did something much better, it sanctified them. To have our wounds ‘sanctified’ could be seen as the deepest kind of healing. In some sense the removal of our scars would be the removal of an integral part of who we are and what we are becoming. That does not mean that we should be define by our scars. However, the way in which we carry them will either mature us and bring glory to God, our dehumanise us by making us bitter and angry. Your scars tell part of your story.

The wounds of Jesus will always remain an integral part of who he is and what he did. The attitude that Jesus carried in his heart, ‘sanctified his scars’; what do we mean by that? We have all got scars, and usually we are ashamed of our scars, we keep them hidden and concealed, they are often ugly and unattractive.

Scars and wounds that were caused by cruelty were transformed by the resurrection into symbols life and hope. Jesus’ scars brought healing to others. They brought assurance to his disciples; they helped to heal their doubt. Our scars may be physical or emotional, but if we have the same loving, forgiving, relationship-healing attitudes as Jesus, then maybe the scars we have received will be used by God to bring healing to others.

Can you grasp how open and vulnerable Jesus must have felt?

It would have been very understandable if Jesus had wanted to protect himself, if he had chosen to be cautious, but he didn’t. He allowed himself to become vulnerable so he could restore his relationship with his disciples. Jesus is teaching us a very important lesson; when we live in the power of his resurrection, free from the mindset of a victim, we will be secure enough to be vulnerable, we will allow our wounds to bring healing to others.

We have no control what wounds we will receive in life, but we have a choice how we carry them.
prayer: Father give me the grace to carry my wounds well
html:
STUDY THEME, The Humanity of Jesus. Part 28

Study Text:

Jesus’ resurrection teaches us that the power of God in our lives will restore and rebuild broken relationships.

Relationships are rescued when our lives are touched with the power of Jesus’ new life. Between his resurrection and ascension, the main focus of Jesus was one of restoring and reassuring. We have no control what wounds we will receive in life, but we have a choice how we carry them.

Prayer: Father give me the grace to carry my wounds well
Bible in a year reading plan: Deuteronomy 32:15-34;12 Joshua 1:1-9
